                                                                                                                • MetaClow: Just Talk - An Agent That Meta-Learns and Evolves in the Wild

                                                                                                                MetaClow：Just Talk —— 一个在野外环境进行元学习与进化的智能体

                                                                                                                Peng Xia 1∗ ,Jianwen Chen 1∗ ,Xinyu Yang 2∗ ,Haoqin Tu 3∗ ,Jiaqi Liu 1∗ ,Kaiwen Xiong 1∗ ,Siwei  Han 1 ,Shi Qiu 1 ,Haonian Ji 1 ,Yuyin Zhou 3 ,Zeyu Zheng 4 ,Cihang Xie 3 ,Huaxiu Yao 1∗ Peng Xia 1∗ ,Jianwen Chen 1∗ ,Xinyu Yang 2∗ ,Haoqin Tu 3∗ ,Jiaqi Liu 1∗ ,Kaiwen Xiong 1∗ ,Siwei  Han 1 ,Shi Qiu 1 ,Haonian Ji 1 ,Yuyin Zhou 3 ,Zeyu Zheng 4 ,Cihang Xie 3 ,Huaxiu Yao 1∗

                                                                                                                1 G UNC-Chapel Hill, 2 G Carnegie Mellon University, 3 G UC Santa Cruz, 4 G UC Berkeley, Core Contributors 1 北卡罗来纳大学教堂山分校，2 卡内基梅隆大学，3 加州大学圣克鲁兹分校，4 加州大学伯克利分校，核心贡献者

                                                                                                                Large language model (LLM) agents have rapidly emerged as powerful assistants for complex, multi-step tasks, yet agents deployed in the wild remain largely static, trained once and served unchanged regardless of how user needs evolve. This creates a fundamental tension: they must serve users continuously without interruption, yet their capabilities grow stale as the task distribution drifts with real-world usage. On platforms such as OpenClaw, where a single agent connects to 20+ messaging channels and handles diverse, evolving workloads, existing approaches either store raw trajectories without distilling transferable behavioral knowledge, maintain static skill libraries disconnected from weight optimization, or incur service downtime during retraining. We present MetaClaw, a continual meta-learning framework that jointly maintains a base LLM policy and an evolving skill library of reusable behavioral instructions, improving both through two complementary mechanisms. Skill-driven fast adaptation analyzes failure trajectories and synthesizes new skills via an LLM evolver, taking effect immediately with zero service downtime. Opportunistic policy optimization performs gradient-based weight updates via cloud LoRA fine-tuning using RL with a process reward model, triggered only during user-inactive windows by the Opportunistic Meta-Learning Scheduler (OMLS), which monitors configurable sleep hours, system keyboard inactivity, and Google Calendar occupancy. The two mechanisms are mutually reinforcing: a better policy produces more informative failures for skill synthesis, and richer skills yield higher-reward trajectories for policy optimization. To prevent stale reward contamination, a skill generation versioning mechanism strictly separates support data (failure trajectories consumed by skill evolution) from query data (post-adaptation trajectories used for RL updates). Built on a proxy-based architecture, MetaClaw scales to production-size LLMs without a local GPU. Experiments on MetaClaw-Bench (934 questions, 44 simulated workdays) and AutoResearchClaw (23-stage autonomous research pipeline) demonstrate consistent improvements: skill-driven adaptation improves accuracy by up to 32% relative; the full pipeline advances Kimi-K2.5 from 21.4% to 40.6% accuracy (vs. GPT-5.2 baseline 41.1%) with an 8.25× gain in end-to-end task completion; and skill injection alone improves AutoResearchClaw composite robustness by 18.3%. 大语言模型（LLM）智能体已迅速成为处理复杂多步任务的强大助手，然而在野外环境中部署的智能体大多保持静态：它们经过一次训练后便不再改变，无论用户需求如何演变。这产生了一个根本性的矛盾：智能体必须持续为用户提供不间断的服务，但随着任务分布随实际使用情况发生偏移，其能力会逐渐过时。在 OpenClaw 等平台上，单个智能体连接着 20 多个消息渠道并处理多样化且不断演变的工作负载，现有的方法要么存储原始轨迹而不提炼可迁移的行为知识，要么维护与权重优化脱节的静态技能库，要么在重新训练期间导致服务中断。我们提出了 MetaClaw，这是一个持续元学习框架，它共同维护一个基础 LLM 策略和一个可重用行为指令的进化技能库，并通过两种互补机制改进两者。基于技能的快速适应机制通过 LLM 进化器分析失败轨迹并合成新技能，实现零服务停机的即时生效。机会主义策略优化通过云端 LoRA 微调，利用带有过程奖励模型的强化学习（RL）进行基于梯度的权重更新，仅在机会主义元学习调度器（OMLS）检测到用户不活跃窗口时触发，该调度器会监控可配置的睡眠时间、系统键盘不活跃状态及 Google 日历占用情况。这两种机制相互促进：更好的策略能为技能合成产生更具信息量的失败案例，而更丰富的技能则能为策略优化提供更高奖励的轨迹。为防止过时奖励的污染，技能生成版本控制机制严格区分了支持数据（用于技能进化的失败轨迹）和查询数据（用于 RL 更新的适应后轨迹）。MetaClaw 基于代理架构构建，无需本地 GPU 即可扩展至生产级 LLM。在 MetaClaw-Bench（934 个问题，44 个模拟工作日）和 AutoResearchClaw（23 阶段自主研究流水线）上的实验证明了其持续的改进：基于技能的适应使准确率相对提升高达 32%；完整流水线将 Kimi-K2.5 的准确率从 21.4% 提升至 40.6%（对比 GPT-5.2 基准的 41.1%），并在端到端任务完成率上实现了 8.25× 的增益；仅技能注入一项就使 AutoResearchClaw 的综合鲁棒性提高了 18.3%。

                                                                                                                Github: https://github.com/aiming-lab/MetaClaw Github: https://github.com/aiming-lab/MetaClaw

                                                                                                                1 Introduction

                                                                                                                1 引言

                                                                                                                Large language model (LLM) agents have demonstrated remarkable capabilities across complex tasks (Yao et al., 2022; Shinn et al., 2023), yet agents deployed in the wild remain largely static, trained once and served unchanged regardless of how the user's needs evolve (Zhang et al., 2025b; Naihin et al., 2023; Song et al.). Consider OpenClaw (OpenClaw, 2026), an open-source CLI agent platform connecting to 20+ messaging channels, where a single user's workload may shift from multi-step file system operations one week to multi-agent messaging workflows the next. As the task distribution drifts, a frozen model becomes increasingly misaligned with actual usage patterns, repeatedly failing on task types underrepresented during pretraining. 大语言模型（LLM）智能体在处理复杂任务时展现出了卓越的能力（Yao et al., 2022; Shinn et al., 2023），然而在野外环境中部署的智能体大多保持静态，无论用户需求如何演变，它们在训练一次后便不再改变（Zhang et al., 2025b; Naihin et al., 2023; Song et al.）。以 OpenClaw（OpenClaw, 2026）为例，这是一个连接 20 多个消息渠道的开源 CLI 智能体平台，单个用户的工作负载可能从一周的多步文件系统操作转变为下一周的多智能体消息工作流。随着任务分布的偏移，冻结的模型会逐渐与实际使用模式不匹配，导致在预训练期间未充分覆盖的任务类型上反复失败。

                                                                                                                Existing approaches to agent adaptation fall into three broad categories, each with notable limitations. Memory-based methods (Shinn et al., 2023; Zhao et al., 2024; Fang et al., 2025; Tang et al., 2025; Ouyang et al., 2025; Chhikara et al., 2025; Liu et al., 2026a) store raw conversation trajectories for future retrieval, but such 现有的智能体适应方法大致可分为三类，每一类都有明显的局限性。基于记忆的方法（Shinn et al., 2023; Zhao et al., 2024; Fang et al., 2025; Tang et al., 2025; Ouyang et al., 2025; Chhikara et al., 2025; Liu et al., 2026a）存储原始对话轨迹以供未来检索，但此类

                                                                                                                 

                                                                                                                Figure 1 Overview of C. MetaClaw. The framework improves the meta-model M=(θ,S) via two complementary loops operating at different timescales. Skill-driven fast adaptation (left) analyzes failed trajectories and instantly expands the skill library S without parameter updates,taking effect immediately for subsequent tasks. Opportunistic policy optimization (right) accumulates post-adaptation trajectories and, once sufficient data is available, leverages idle signals (sleep, inactivity, calendar) detected by the Opportunistic Meta-Learning Scheduler to trigger RL-based weight updates on θ via Cloud LoRA fine-tuning. 图 1：MetaClaw 概览。该框架通过在不同时间尺度上运行的两个互补循环来改进元模型 M=(θ,S)。基于技能的快速适应（左侧）分析失败轨迹并即时扩展技能库 S，无需更新参数，即可对后续任务立即生效。机会主义策略优化（右侧）积累适应后的轨迹，并在数据充足时，利用机会主义元学习调度器检测到的空闲信号（睡眠、不活跃、日历）触发基于云端 LoRA 微调的 θ 强化学习权重更新。

                                                                                                                trajectories are verbose and redundant, preventing the agent from extracting transferable behavioral patterns. Skill-based methods (Xia et al., 2026; Zhang et al., 2025a, 2026b; Wu et al., 2025; Zhang et al., 2026a) compress experience into reusable behavioral instructions, yet treat the resulting skill library as a static database never coordinated with weight optimization. RL-based methods (Schulman et al., 2017; Ahmadian et al., 2024; Shao et al., 2024; Feng et al., 2025; Zheng et al., 2025) update model weights, but operate in small-scale or offline settings and ignore a critical data validity problem: once skills have evolved, trajectories collected under the old skill context carry stale rewards that contaminate gradient updates if reused without filtration. A common thread across all three categories is that each addresses only one aspect of adaptation in isolation, leaving the complementary dimensions unexploited. 轨迹冗长且重复，阻碍了智能体提取可迁移的行为模式。基于技能的方法（Xia et al., 2026; Zhang et al., 2025a, 2026b; Wu et al., 2025; Zhang et al., 2026a）将经验压缩为可重用的行为指令，但将生成的技能库视为静态数据库，从未与权重优化进行协调。基于强化学习的方法（Schulman et al., 2017; Ahmadian et al., 2024; Shao et al., 2024; Feng et al., 2025; Zheng et al., 2025）更新模型权重，但仅在小规模或离线环境下运行，且忽略了一个关键的数据有效性问题：一旦技能发生演变，在旧技能背景下收集的轨迹会带有陈旧的奖励，若不经筛选直接重用，会污染梯度更新。这三类方法共同的问题在于，它们都仅孤立地解决了适应性的一个方面，而未利用其互补维度。

                                                                                                                Our key observation is that two fundamentally different timescales of adaptation are in fact naturally complementary. Behavioral heuristics (e.g., "always verify a file path before reading," "confirm before destructive commands") can be distilled within seconds from a single failed conversation and injected immediately as skill instructions. Improving the model's underlying policy across diverse task types requires gradient-based optimization over many trajectories, on a timescale of minutes to hours. The two mechanisms are also mutually reinforcing: a better policy produces more informative failures for skill synthesis, and richer skills yield higher-reward trajectories for policy optimization. No existing system unifies these two forms of adaptation into a coherent framework that exploits this virtuous cycle. 我们的核心观察是，两种本质上不同的适应时间尺度实际上是天然互补的。行为启发式规则（例如“读取前务必验证文件路径”、“执行破坏性命令前需确认”）可以在单次失败对话后的几秒内提炼出来，并立即作为技能指令注入。而跨多种任务类型改进模型的基础策略，则需要对大量轨迹进行基于梯度的优化，时间尺度在几分钟到几小时不等。这两种机制也是相互促进的：更好的策略能为技能合成产生更具信息量的失败案例，而更丰富的技能则能为策略优化提供更高奖励的轨迹。目前尚无现有系统能将这两种适应形式统一为一个利用此良性循环的连贯框架。

                                                                                                                We present MetaClaw, a continual meta-learning (Finn et al., 2017; Yao et al., 2021) framework that jointly maintains a base LLM policy and an evolving skill library of reusable behavioral instructions. The skill library serves a dual role: as a meta-parameter that accumulates behavioral knowledge across the task stream, and as an adaptation basis from which task-specific skills are retrieved at inference time. MetaClaw improves both components through two mechanisms. Skill-driven fast adaptation performs gradient-free skill evolution: an LLM analyzes failure trajectories and synthesizes new behavioral instructions (Xia et al., 2026) that take effect immediately with zero service downtime. Opportunistic policy optimization uses RL with a process reward model (PRM) (Zhang et al., 2025c) to update model weights via cloud (TML, 2026) LoRA fine-tuning (Hu et al., 2021), optimizing post-adaptation performance. Two design principles govern their coordination. First, when to run policy optimization: our Opportunistic Meta-Learning Scheduler (OMLS) monitors three idle signals, i.e., configurable sleep hours, system keyboard inactivity, and Google Calendar event occupancy, and triggers weight updates only during user-inactive windows, eliminating downtime. Second, which data to use: we distinguish support data (failure trajectories consumed by skill evolution) from query data (trajectories collected after new skills take effect). Only query data, reflecting the agent's post-adaptation behavior, is valid for RL; support data carries rewards conditioned on the old skill context and is excluded. Our skill generation versioning mechanism enforces this separation by stamping each trajectory with its skill generation index and flushing stale samples from the training buffer whenever skills evolve. 我们提出了 MetaClaw，这是一个持续元学习（Finn et al., 2017; Yao et al., 2021）框架，旨在共同维护基础 LLM 策略和一个可重用行为指令的演进技能库。技能库发挥双重作用：作为跨任务流积累行为知识的元参数，以及作为推理时检索任务特定技能的适应基础。MetaClaw 通过两种机制改进这两个组件。基于技能的快速适应执行无梯度技能演进：LLM 分析失败轨迹并合成新的行为指令（Xia et al., 2026），实现零服务停机时间的即时生效。机会主义策略优化利用带有过程奖励模型（PRM）（Zhang et al., 2025c）的强化学习，通过云端（TML, 2026）LoRA 微调（Hu et al., 2021）更新模型权重，从而优化适应后的性能。它们的协调遵循两条设计原则。首先，何时运行策略优化：我们的机会主义元学习调度器（OMLS）监控三种空闲信号，即用户可配置的睡眠时间、系统键盘不活跃状态以及 Google 日历事件占用情况，仅在用户不活跃窗口触发权重更新，从而消除停机时间。其次，使用哪些数据：我们将支持数据（被技能演进消耗的失败轨迹）与查询数据（新技能生效后收集的轨迹）区分开来。只有反映智能体适应后行为的查询数据才对强化学习有效；支持数据携带的是基于旧技能背景的奖励，因此被排除在外。我们的技能生成版本控制机制通过为每条轨迹打上技能生成索引戳，并在技能演进时清除训练缓冲区中的陈旧样本，从而强制执行这种分离。

                                                                                                                In summary, our primary contribution is MetaClaw, a continual meta-learning framework that unifies skill-driven fast adaptation with opportunistic policy optimization, enabling deployed LLM agents to evolve continuously through a proxy-based architecture without requiring a local GPU. We evaluate on MetaClaw-Bench, a new benchmark of 934 questions over 44 simulated workdays, where each day forms a sequential, feedback-driven multi-round session of real CLI tasks (file editing, JSON structuring, shell scripting). Experiments with GPT-5.2 and Kimi-K2.5 show that skill-driven fast adaptation alone improves overall accuracy by up to 32.2% in relative terms; MetaClaw (Full) further advances Kimi-K2.5 from 21.4% to 40.6%, improves end-to-end task completion by 8.25× on Part I and file-check completion by 185% on Part II,and nearly closes the gap with GPT-5.2’s baseline. We further validate on AutoResearchClaw, a 23-stage autonomous research pipeline, where skill injection alone improves the composite robustness score by 18.3%, demonstrating cross-domain generalization of MetaClaw's adaptation mechanisms. 总之，我们的主要贡献是 MetaClaw，这是一个持续元学习框架，它将基于技能的快速适应与机会主义策略优化相结合，使部署的 LLM 智能体能够通过基于代理（proxy-based）的架构持续进化，而无需本地 GPU。我们在 MetaClaw-Bench 上进行了评估，这是一个包含 44 个模拟工作日、共 934 个问题的新基准测试，每一天都构成了一个序列化的、反馈驱动的真实 CLI 任务多轮会话（文件编辑、JSON 结构化、Shell 脚本编写）。使用 GPT-5.2 和 Kimi-K2.5 的实验表明，仅基于技能的快速适应就能使整体准确率相对提升高达 32.2%；MetaClaw（完整版）进一步将 Kimi-K2.5 的准确率从 21.4% 提升至 40.6%，将第一部分的端到端任务完成率提升了 8.25×，将第二部分的文件检查完成率提升了 185%，并几乎缩小了与 GPT-5.2 基准表现的差距。我们进一步在 AutoResearchClaw（一个 23 阶段的自主研究流水线）上进行了验证，仅技能注入就使综合鲁棒性得分提高了 18.3%，证明了 MetaClaw 适应机制的跨领域泛化能力。

                                                                                                                2 Problem Setup

                                                                                                                2 问题设置

                                                                                                                We consider a deployed CLI agent that serves a user over a stream of tasks τ1,τ2,… drawn from a non-stationary distribution pt(τ) . Each task τi consists of a user instruction and environmental context (file system state, shell history,etc.),and the agent must produce a sequence of actions a1:T to accomplish the task. The agent’s behavior at any point in time is fully determined by a meta-model: 我们考虑一个部署在 CLI 环境中的智能体，它通过任务流 τ1,τ2,… 为用户提供服务，这些任务服从非平稳分布 pt(τ)。每个任务 τi 由用户指令和环境上下文（文件系统状态、Shell 历史记录等）组成，智能体必须生成一系列动作 a1:T 来完成任务。智能体在任何时间点的行为完全由元模型决定：

                                                                                                                (1)M=(θ,S),

                                                                                                                 

                                                                                                                where θ denotes the parameters of the base LLM policy and S={s1,s2,…,sK} is a library of skill instructions, i.e., concise, reusable behavioral directives injected into the agent's system prompt at inference time. Given a task τ ,the agent generates actions according to: 其中 θ 表示基础 LLM 策略的参数，S={s1,s2,…,sK} 是技能指令库，即在推理时注入智能体系统提示词中的简洁、可重用的行为指令。给定任务 τ，智能体根据以下方式生成动作：

                                                                                                                (2)a∼πθ(⋅∣τ,RETRIEVE(S,τ)),

                                                                                                                 

                                                                                                                where RETRIEVE (S,τ)⊆S selects the most relevant skills for the current task via embedding-based retrieval. 其中 RETRIEVE (S,τ)⊆S 通过基于嵌入的检索为当前任务选择最相关的技能。

                                                                                                                The meta-model M evolves over the task stream as the agent accumulates experience. We distinguish two types of trajectory data based on their role in this evolution. Support data D sup  consists of trajectories whose failures drive adaptation of the skill library S ; these trajectories are consumed by the adaptation process and reflect pre-adaptation behavior. Query data D qry  consists of trajectories collected after adaptation has taken effect; these reflect the agent's post-adaptation behavior and are used to optimize the policy parameters θ . Maintaining a strict separation between support and query data is essential: mixing them would cause θ to be optimized against stale reward signals that no longer reflect the agent's current capabilities. 元模型 M 随着智能体积累经验在任务流中不断进化。我们根据轨迹数据在进化中的作用将其分为两类。支持数据 D sup  由其失败案例驱动技能库 S 适应的轨迹组成；这些轨迹被适应过程消耗，反映了适应前的行为。查询数据 D qry  由适应生效后收集的轨迹组成；它们反映了智能体适应后的行为，并用于优化策略参数 θ。保持支持数据和查询数据的严格分离至关重要：将它们混合会导致 θ 在过时的奖励信号上进行优化，而这些信号已不再反映智能体当前的能力。

                                                                                                                The goal of MetaClaw is to continuously improve M over the task stream,not merely to solve each task in isolation, but to become progressively better at adapting to new tasks as they arrive. This positions MetaClaw as a continual meta-learning system: the agent learns from a non-stationary task stream while simultaneously improving its own adaptation capability. MetaClaw 的目标是在任务流中持续改进 M，不仅是为了孤立地解决每个任务，而是为了在面对新任务时能够逐步提高适应能力。这使 MetaClaw 成为一个持续元学习系统：智能体在从非平稳任务流中学习的同时，也在不断提升自身的适应能力。

                                                                                                                3 MetaClaw

                                                                                                                3 MetaClaw

                                                                                                                3.1 Overview

                                                                                                                3.1 概述

                                                                                                                MetaClaw improves the meta-model M=(θ,S) through two complementary mechanisms operating at different timescales (Figure 1). Skill-driven fast adaptation analyzes failure trajectories and synthesizes new skill instructions that are immediately injected into the agent’s prompt,evolving S without touching model weights. Opportunistic policy optimization uses post-adaptation trajectories to update θ via reinforcement learning, deferred to user-inactive windows by the Opportunistic Meta-Learning Scheduler (OMLS). A skill generation versioning mechanism ensures that policy optimization always trains on query data collected under the current skill library, preventing stale reward contamination from support data. The two mechanisms are mutually reinforcing: a better θ produces more informative failures for skill synthesis,and richer skills produce higher-reward trajectories for policy optimization. This virtuous cycle enables the system to learn to become better at adapting. The complete procedure is summarized in Algorithm 1. MetaClaw 通过在不同时间尺度上运行的两种互补机制来改进元模型 M=(θ,S)（图 1）。基于技能的快速适应分析失败轨迹并合成新的技能指令，这些指令会立即注入到智能体的提示词中，从而在不触动模型权重的情况下进化 S。机会主义策略优化利用适应后的轨迹，通过强化学习更新 θ，并由机会主义元学习调度器（OMLS）推迟到用户不活跃的时间窗口执行。技能生成版本控制机制确保策略优化始终在当前技能库下收集的查询数据上进行训练，防止过时的奖励污染支持数据。这两种机制相互促进：更好的 θ 会产生更具信息量的失败案例以用于技能合成，而更丰富的技能会为策略优化产生更高奖励的轨迹。这种良性循环使系统能够学会如何更好地进行适应。完整流程总结在算法 1 中。

                                                                                                                3.2 Skill-Driven Fast Adaptation

                                                                                                                3.2 基于技能的快速适应

                                                                                                                Given the current meta-model (θ,Sg) ,the agent executes tasks and collects trajectories. Trajectories that reveal failure modes form the support set Dg sup  . Skill-driven adaptation evolves the skill library via a gradient-free experience distillation process: 给定当前元模型 (θ,Sg)，智能体执行任务并收集轨迹。揭示失败模式的轨迹构成支持集 Dg sup 。基于技能的适应通过无梯度经验蒸馏过程演化技能库：

                                                                                                                (3)Sg+1=Sg∪E(Sg,Dg sup ),

                                                                                                                 

                                                                                                                where E is a skill evolver,an LLM that analyzes failure trajectories and synthesizes new behavioral instructions. The index g denotes the skill generation,incremented each time the library changes. This step modifies only S , leaving θ fixed,and takes effect immediately for all subsequent tasks. Because skill injection operates through the prompt rather than model parameters, fast adaptation incurs zero service downtime. 其中 E 是技能演化器，即一个分析失败轨迹并合成新行为指令的 LLM。索引 g 表示技能生成版本，每次库发生变化时都会递增。此步骤仅修改 S，保持 θ 不变，并对所有后续任务立即生效。由于技能注入是通过提示词而非模型参数进行的，因此快速适应不会产生服务停机时间。

                                                                                                                This mechanism is gradient-free by design,not by approximation. The skill library S lives in a discrete natural-language space where gradient descent is ill-defined; LLM-based failure analysis is the natural adaptation mechanism for this space. 该机制在设计上就是无梯度的，而非近似处理。技能库 S 存在于离散的自然语言空间中，梯度下降在此处定义不明确；基于 LLM 的故障分析是该空间中自然的适应机制。

                                                                                                                The skill library S plays a dual role in the learning structure. As a meta-parameter, S accumulates behavioral knowledge across the entire task stream,with each skill generation Sg+1⊇Sg representing the system’s growing operational knowledge. As an adaptation basis, RETRIEVE (S,τ) extracts a task-specific subset at inference time, providing instant specialization without any parameter update. This dual character arises because natural-language instructions are inherently cross-task transferable: a skill distilled from one failure (e.g., "verify file path before reading") generalizes to all tasks involving file operations. Unlike systems where task-specific adaptations are ephemeral and discarded after each task, each adaptation episode in MetaClaw contributes lasting knowledge to the meta-model, making knowledge accumulation a feature rather than a side effect. 技能库 S 在学习结构中扮演双重角色。作为元参数，S 在整个任务流中积累行为知识，每个技能生成版本 Sg+1⊇Sg 都代表了系统不断增长的操作知识。作为适应基础，RETRIEVE (S,τ) 在推理时提取特定任务的子集，无需任何参数更新即可提供即时专业化。这种双重特性源于自然语言指令天生具有跨任务迁移性：从一次失败中提炼出的技能（例如“读取前验证文件路径”）可推广至所有涉及文件操作的任务。与那些任务特定适应是短暂的、在任务结束后即被丢弃的系统不同，MetaClaw 中的每次适应片段都会为元模型贡献持久的知识，使知识积累成为一种特性而非副作用。

                                                                                                                3.3 Opportunistic Policy Optimization

                                                                                                                3.3 机会主义策略优化

                                                                                                                After each skill-driven adaptation step, the agent continues serving tasks under the latest skill library. Because policy optimization is deferred to idle windows, the skill library may have advanced through several generations by the time training begins. Let g∗ denote the current skill generation when a training window opens. The RL buffer B accumulates query trajectories across all post-adaptation generations,and policy optimization updates θ over this buffer: 在每次基于技能的适应步骤后，智能体继续在最新的技能库下处理任务。由于策略优化被推迟到空闲窗口，当训练开始时，技能库可能已经演进了多个版本。设 g∗ 为训练窗口开启时的当前技能生成版本。RL 缓冲区 B 积累了所有适应后版本的查询轨迹，策略优化在此缓冲区上更新 θ：

                                                                                                                (4)θt+1=θt+α∇θE(τ,ξ,g′)∼B[R(πθ(⋅∣τ,Sg′))],

                                                                                                                 

                                                                                                                where g′≤g∗ is the skill generation under which each trajectory was collected,and R is a process reward model (PRM) score. The versioning mechanism (Section 3.4) guarantees that B contains only query data,i.e.,every sample reflects post-adaptation behavior under its respective skill generation. Crucially, policy optimization does not optimize θ for raw task performance,but for how well the agent performs after skill adaptation. A better θ yields a meta-model from which skill-driven adaptation produces stronger post-adaptation behavior, resulting in an improved meta-model M′=(θt+1,Sg∗) . 其中 g′≤g∗ 是收集每条轨迹时所处的技能生成版本，R 是过程奖励模型 (PRM) 分数。版本控制机制（第 3.4 节）确保 B 仅包含查询数据，即每个样本都反映了其各自技能生成版本下的适应后行为。至关重要的是，策略优化并非为了原始任务性能而优化 θ，而是为了优化智能体在技能适应后的表现。更好的 θ 会产生一个元模型，基于该模型，基于技能的适应能产生更强的适应后行为，从而得到改进的元模型 M′=(θt+1,Sg∗)。

                                                                                                                In practice, policy optimization is realized via cloud LoRA fine-tuning using GRPO, deferred to idle windows by the Opportunistic Meta-Learning Scheduler (Section 3.5). Importantly, training is initiated only after the query buffer B has accumulated a sufficient number of trajectories; launching RL with too few samples leads to high-variance gradient estimates and unstable policy updates. This means policy optimization naturally lags behind skill-driven adaptation by days or longer, reinforcing the asymmetry between the two timescales: skills evolve continuously, while the policy improves in discrete, data-gated steps. 在实践中，策略优化通过使用 GRPO 的云端 LoRA 微调实现，并由机会主义元学习调度器（第 3.5 节）推迟到空闲窗口执行。重要的是，只有在查询缓冲区 B 积累了足够数量的轨迹后才会启动训练；使用过少的样本启动 RL 会导致高方差的梯度估计和不稳定的策略更新。这意味着策略优化在时间上自然滞后于基于技能的适应数天或更久，从而强化了两者之间的时间尺度不对称性：技能持续演化，而策略则在离散的、数据门控的步骤中改进。

                                                                                                                3.4 Skill Generation Versioning

                                                                                                                3.4 技能生成版本控制

                                                                                                                The support-query separation defined in Section 2 must be enforced in MetaClaw's online setting, where tasks arrive sequentially and skill evolution is triggered asynchronously. Without a dedicated mechanism, support data can leak into the policy optimization buffer. 第 2 节中定义的支持-查询分离必须在 MetaClaw 的在线设置中强制执行，在该设置中任务按顺序到达，且技能演化是异步触发的。如果没有专门的机制，支持数据可能会泄露到策略优化缓冲区中。

                                                                                                                The problem is concrete: a trajectory (τi,ξi) that triggers skill evolution from Sg to Sg+1 carries a reward ri reflecting performance under Sg ,before the new skill existed. If this trajectory enters the RL buffer,policy optimization receives a gradient that penalizes θ for a failure that skill-driven adaptation has already corrected, optimizing for pre-adaptation rather than post-adaptation performance and violating the meta-learning objective in Eq. 4. 问题很具体：触发从 Sg 到 Sg+1 技能演化的轨迹 (τi,ξi)，其携带的奖励 ri 反映的是新技能出现前在 Sg 下的表现。如果将该轨迹存入强化学习缓冲区，策略优化过程接收到的梯度会惩罚 θ，而这本是技能驱动的自适应已经纠正过的失败。这导致优化目标偏向于自适应前的表现，而非自适应后的表现，从而违背了公式 4 中的元学习目标。

                                                                                                                We enforce separation via a skill generation version gi stamped on each collected sample: 我们通过在每个采集样本上标记技能生成版本 gi 来强制实现这种分离：

                                                                                                                • Support set Dg sup  : trajectories collected under Sg whose failures trigger skill evolution Sg→Sg+1 . These are consumed by the skill evolver and discarded from the RL buffer.

                                                                                                                • 支持集 Dg sup ：在 Sg 下采集的轨迹，其失败触发了技能演化 Sg→Sg+1。这些轨迹由技能演化器消耗，并从强化学习缓冲区中丢弃。

                                                                                                                • Query set Dg+1 qry  : trajectories collected after Sg+1 takes effect. Only these,reflecting the agent’s post-adaptation behavior, are eligible for policy optimization gradient updates.

                                                                                                                • 查询集 Dg+1 qry ：在 Sg+1 生效后采集的轨迹。只有这些反映智能体自适应后行为的轨迹，才有资格用于策略优化的梯度更新。

                                                                                                                When the skill generation counter advances from g to g+1 ,the trainer flushes all samples with version ≤g from its buffer. This ensures policy optimization always updates θ with respect to the agent’s adapted behavior, preserving the integrity of the meta-learning structure. 当技能生成计数器从 g 推进到 g+1 时，训练器会从缓冲区中清除所有版本为 ≤g 的样本。这确保了策略优化始终针对智能体的自适应行为更新 θ，从而保持了元学习结构的完整性。

                                                                                                                3.5 Opportunistic Meta-Learning Scheduler

                                                                                                                3.5 机会主义元学习调度器

                                                                                                                Policy optimization requires a model weight hot-swap upon completion, which briefly interrupts inference. In a deployed interactive system, this creates a tension: policy optimization must run periodically to improve θ ,but it must not degrade the user's experience. 策略优化需要在完成后进行模型权重热切换，这会短暂中断推理。在部署的交互式系统中，这产生了一种矛盾：策略优化必须定期运行以改进 θ，但绝不能降低用户体验。

                                                                                                                We introduce the Opportunistic Meta-Learning Scheduler (OMLS), a background daemon that defers policy optimization to periods when the user is not actively interacting with the agent. OMLS monitors three complementary idle signals: 我们引入了机会主义元学习调度器（OMLS），这是一个后台守护进程，将策略优化推迟到用户未与智能体主动交互的时段。OMLS 监控三个互补的空闲信号：

                                                                                                                (1) Sleep window. The user configures a sleep schedule (e.g., 23:00-07:00). During this window, the system is guaranteed to be idle, providing the largest contiguous training block. (1) 休眠窗口。用户配置休眠时间表（例如 23:00-07:00）。在此窗口期间，系统保证处于空闲状态，可提供最长的连续训练块。

                                                                                                                (2) System inactivity. OMLS polls the operating system's input device idle timer (e.g., ioreg HIDIdleTime on macOS). If no keyboard or mouse activity is detected for δ minutes (default: 30),a training window opens. Upon renewed input, the trainer pauses gracefully via mid-batch checkpointing. (2) 系统不活跃状态。OMLS 轮询操作系统的输入设备空闲计时器（例如 macOS 上的 ioreg HIDIdleTime）。如果 δ 分钟（默认：30）内未检测到键盘或鼠标活动，则开启训练窗口。一旦检测到新的输入，训练器通过批次间检查点优雅地暂停。

                                                                                                                (3) Calendar-aware scheduling. OMLS queries the user's Google Calendar API. When the current time falls within a scheduled meeting, the user is presumed unavailable, opening an opportunistic training window. This is the most anticipatory of the three signals: it leverages the user's own schedule to predict idle periods proactively. (3) 日历感知调度。OMLS 查询用户的 Google 日历 API。当当前时间处于预定会议期间时，假定用户不可用，从而开启机会主义训练窗口。这是三个信号中最具预见性的：它利用用户自己的日程安排来主动预测空闲时段。

                                                                                                                A training window opens when any signal indicates user absence and closes when any signal indicates the user has returned. The RL trainer supports pause/resume across fragmented idle windows, accumulating gradient steps opportunistically without requiring a single long contiguous block. 当任何信号表明用户不在时，训练窗口开启；当任何信号表明用户返回时，训练窗口关闭。强化学习训练器支持在碎片化的空闲窗口间暂停/恢复，从而在无需单个长连续块的情况下，机会主义地累积梯度步数。

                                                                                                                4 Experiments

                                                                                                                4 实验

                                                                                                                4.1 Experimental Setup

                                                                                                                4.1 实验设置

                                                                                                                4.1.1 Benchmark and Evaluation Platform

                                                                                                                4.1.1 基准测试与评估平台

                                                                                                                MetaClaw-Bench. We construct MetaClaw-Bench, a continual agentic benchmark comprising two complementary evaluation parts (934 questions total across 44 simulated workdays) for evaluating an agent's ability to adapt across a sequential stream of real-world CLI tasks. Existing agent benchmarks present tasks as independent episodes, providing no mechanism to assess whether an agent improves from accumulated experience. MetaClaw-Bench addresses this gap by structuring evaluation as multi-workday simulations in which the agent operates under consistent workspace and policy rulesets that evolve through user feedback. MetaClaw-Bench。我们构建了 MetaClaw-Bench，这是一个持续性智能体基准测试，包含两个互补的评估部分（在 44 个模拟工作日内共 934 个问题），用于评估智能体在真实 CLI 任务序列流中进行自适应的能力。现有的智能体基准测试将任务视为独立的片段，没有提供评估智能体是否能从积累的经验中改进的机制。MetaClaw-Bench 通过将评估结构化为多工作日模拟来填补这一空白，其中智能体在一致的工作空间和策略规则集下运行，并随用户反馈而演进。

                                                                                                                1. Part I structures evaluation as a 30-workday simulation (346 questions, days 01-30, 10-15 per day). The workspace state (files, configs, project records) persists across rounds within each day, and each question includes the evaluation outcome of the previous round as corrective feedback context. Questions fall into two types:

                                                                                                                2. 第一部分将评估构建为 30 个工作日的模拟（346 个问题，第 01-30 天，每天 10-15 个）。工作区状态（文件、配置、项目记录）在每天的各轮次间保持持久化，且每个问题都包含上一轮的评估结果作为纠错反馈上下文。问题分为两类：

                                                                                                                Algorithm 1 MetaClaw: Continual Meta-Learning for Deployed LLM Agents 算法 1 MetaClaw：部署型 LLM 智能体的持续元学习

                                                                                                                 

                                                                                                                Require: Meta-model M=(θ0,S0) ,skill evolver E ,task stream {τi} ,PRM R ,OMLS idle detector 输入：元模型 M=(θ0,S0)，技能演化器 E，任务流 {τi}，PRM R，OMLS 空闲检测器

                                                                                                                Ensure: Continuously improved meta-model M 输出：持续改进的元模型 M

                                                                                                                    Initialize skill generation g←0 ,RL buffer B←∅     初始化技能生成 g←0，RL 缓冲区 B←∅

                                                                                                                        for each task τi in stream do         对于流中的每个任务 τi 执行：

                                                                                                                            ⊳ Serve task with current meta-model             ⊳ 使用当前元模型处理任务

                                                                                                                            Sτi←RETRIEVE(Sg,τi) // retrieve relevant skills             Sτi←RETRIEVE(Sg,τi) // 检索相关技能

                                                                                                                            ξi←EXECUTE(πθ(⋅∣τi,Sτi)) // collect trajectory             ξi←EXECUTE(πθ(⋅∣τi,Sτi)) // 收集轨迹

                                                                                                                            ri←R(ξi) ; stamp (τi,ξi,ri) with generation g             ri←R(ξi) ；使用生成版本 g 标记 (τi,ξi,ri)

                                                                                                                            if ξi reveals failure then             如果 ξi 显示失败则：

                                                                                                                                Add (τi,ξi) to support set Dg sup                  将 (τi,ξi) 添加到支持集 Dg sup 

                                                                                                                            else             否则：

                                                                                                                                Add (τi,ξi,ri,g) to RL buffer B                 将 (τi,ξi,ri,g) 添加到 RL 缓冲区 B

                                                                                                                            end if             结束如果

                                                                                                                            ⊳ Skill-driven fast adaptation (when failures accumulate)             ⊳ 技能驱动的快速适应（当失败累积时）

                                                                                                                            if |Dg sup |≥ threshold then             if |Dg sup |≥ 阈值 then

                                                                                                                                ΔS←E(Sg,Dg sup ) // synthesize new skills from failures                 ΔS←E(Sg,Dg sup ) // 从失败中合成新技能

                                                                                                                                Sg+1←Sg∪ΔS // evolve skill library                 Sg+1←Sg∪ΔS // 演化技能库

                                                                                                                                Flush all samples with version ≤g from B // support-query separation                 清除 B 中所有版本为 ≤g 的样本 // 支持集-查询集分离

                                                                                                                                g←g+1

                                                                                                                 

                                                                                                                            end if             end if

                                                                                                                            ⊳ Opportunistic policy optimization (when user is idle)             ⊳ 机会主义策略优化（用户空闲时）

                                                                                                                            if OMLS detects idle window and |B|≥ batch size then             if OMLS 检测到空闲窗口且 |B|≥ 批次大小 then

                                                                                                                                θ←θ+α∇θE(τ,ξ,r,g′)∼B[R(πθ(⋅∣τ,Sg′))] // RL update                 θ←θ+α∇θE(τ,ξ,r,g′)∼B[R(πθ(⋅∣τ,Sg′))] // RL 更新

                                                                                                                                Hot-swap model weights // deploy updated θ                 热切换模型权重 // 部署更新后的 θ

                                                                                                                            end if             end if

                                                                                                                        end for         end for

                                                                                                                 

                                                                                                                file-check tasks (structured edits or transformations producing output files validated by automated checkers) and multi-choice tasks (conceptual procedural questions on domain-specific rules). Task difficulty increases monotonically with day index, with days 25-30 requiring sophisticated multi-step reasoning. Part I's file-check tasks are heavily execution-oriented, with many interdependent side effects, providing a conservative measure of end-to-end completion. 文件检查任务（通过自动化检查器验证输出文件的结构化编辑或转换）和多项选择任务（关于领域特定规则的概念性程序问题）。任务难度随日期索引单调递增，第 25-30 天的任务需要复杂的逐步推理。第一部分的任务高度侧重于执行，具有许多相互依赖的副作用，提供了端到端完成情况的保守衡量标准。

                                                                                                                1. Part II extends the evaluation to a 14-workday simulation (588 questions, 42 per day: 434 multi-choice and 154 file-check). Part II's file-check tasks are rule-based transformations where compliance with behavioral heuristics (e.g., schema conventions, timestamp formats) is the primary bottleneck, making them more amenable to skill distillation. This design provides a complementary signal: while Part I stress-tests execution reliability, Part II directly measures how quickly the RL-trained policy internalizes procedural rules across a higher-density task stream.

                                                                                                                2. 第二部分将评估扩展至 14 个工作日的模拟（588 个问题，每天 42 个：434 个多项选择题和 154 个文件检查任务）。第二部分的文件检查任务是基于规则的转换，其主要瓶颈在于对行为启发式规则（如模式约定、时间戳格式）的合规性，这使其更易于进行技能蒸馏。该设计提供了互补信号：第一部分压力测试执行可靠性，而第二部分直接衡量 RL 训练的策略在更高密度的任务流中内化程序规则的速度。

                                                                                                                We report two primary metrics across both parts: overall accuracy (mean per-question score) and file-check completion rate (fraction of file-check outputs passing all automated checker assertions simultaneously). Because the benchmark tasks are authored to simulate realistic deployment rather than collected from actual user sessions, we view both parts as controlled stress tests of continual adaptation under increasing difficulty. 我们报告了两个部分的主要指标：总体准确率（每个问题的平均得分）和文件检查完成率（同时通过所有自动化检查器断言的文件检查输出比例）。由于基准任务旨在模拟真实部署而非收集自实际用户会话，我们将两部分均视为在难度递增下进行持续适应的受控压力测试。

                                                                                                                Downstream evaluation: AutoResearchClaw. To test whether MetaClaw's adaptation mechanisms generalize beyond CLI-task benchmarks, we additionally evaluate on AutoResearchClaw (Liu et al., 2026b), a fully autonomous 23-stage research pipeline that transforms a single research idea into a conference-ready paper, covering literature search, hypothesis generation, experiment design, code synthesis, sandbox execution, result analysis, paper drafting, and multi-agent peer review. Unlike MetaClaw-Bench's structured file-check and multi-choice tasks, AutoResearchClaw presents an open-ended, long-horizon agentic workload where failures manifest as stage retries, excessive refinement cycles, and incomplete pipeline runs. We report four pipeline-level metrics: stage retry rate, refine cycle count, pipeline stage completion (out of 19 scorable stages), and a composite robustness score (weighted average of stage completion rate at 40%, retry reduction at 30%, and refine cycle efficiency at 30%). 下游评估：AutoResearchClaw。为测试 MetaClaw 的适应机制是否能推广至 CLI 任务基准之外，我们额外在 AutoResearchClaw (Liu et al., 2026b) 上进行了评估，这是一个完全自主的 23 阶段研究流水线，可将单一研究构思转化为会议论文，涵盖文献搜索、假设生成、实验设计、代码合成、沙盒执行、结果分析、论文撰写及多智能体同行评审。与 MetaClaw-Bench 的结构化文件检查和多项选择任务不同，AutoResearchClaw 呈现出开放式、长周期的智能体工作负载，其失败表现为阶段重试、过度的细化循环以及流水线运行不完整。我们报告了四个流水线级指标：阶段重试率、细化循环次数、流水线阶段完成度（共 19 个可评分阶段）以及综合鲁棒性得分（阶段完成率占 40%、重试减少率占 30%、细化循环效率占 30% 的加权平均值）。

                                                                                                                Baselines and Implementation Details. We evaluate two frontier LLMs as backbone policies: GPT- 基线与实现细节。我们评估了两个前沿大语言模型作为骨干策略：GPT-

                                                                                                                Table 1 Main results on MetaClaw-Bench Parts I and II. Acc.: mean per-question accuracy. Compl.: file-check completion rate. MetaClaw (Full) is evaluated for Kimi-K2.5 only. Best result per model per part is bolded. 表 1 MetaClaw-Bench 第一部分和第二部分的主要结果。Acc.：每题平均准确率。Compl.：文件检查完成率。MetaClaw (Full) 仅针对 Kimi-K2.5 进行评估。各模型在各部分的最佳结果已加粗。

                                                                                                                ModelConditionPart I (30 days, 346 Q)Part II (14 days, 588 Q)
                                                                                                                Acc. (%)Compl. (%)Acc. (%)Compl. (%)
                                                                                                                GPT-5.2Baseline41.114.744.958.4
                                                                                                                GPT-5.2MetaClaw (Skills)44.017.149.167.5
                                                                                                                Kimi-K2.5Baseline21.42.021.118.2
                                                                                                                Kimi-K2.5MetaClaw (Skills)28.32.026.933.8
                                                                                                                Kimi-K2.5MetaClaw (Full)40.616.539.651.9
                                                                                                                模型条件第一部分（30天，346个问题）第二部分（14天，588个问题）
                                                                                                                准确率 (%)完成率 (%)准确率 (%)完成率 (%)
                                                                                                                GPT-5.2基线41.114.744.958.4
                                                                                                                GPT-5.2MetaClaw（技能）44.017.149.167.5
                                                                                                                Kimi-K2.5基线21.42.021.118.2
                                                                                                                Kimi-K2.5MetaClaw（技能）28.32.026.933.8
                                                                                                                Kimi-K2.5MetaClaw（完整版）40.616.539.651.9

                                                                                                                5.2 (OpenAI, 2025) and Kimi-K2.5 (Team et al., 2026). We compare three conditions: 1) Baseline: the base model served without any adaptation mechanism. 2) MetaClaw (Skills): the base model augmented with skill-driven fast adaptation; after each failed trajectory, the skill evolver synthesizes behavioral instructions immediately injected into the system prompt,with top- k retrieval via cosine similarity over sentence embeddings. 3) MetaClaw (Full): the full pipeline combining skill-driven fast adaptation with opportunistic policy optimization via RL (5-day training run), evaluated for Kimi-K2.5 only, as it requires a cloud LoRA training endpoint configured for the target backbone. All conditions use identical prompts and tool sets. This design isolates the individual contributions of the two MetaClaw components as defined in Section 3. 5.2 (OpenAI, 2025) 和 Kimi-K2.5 (Team et al., 2026)。我们比较了三种条件：1) 基线：不使用任何适应机制的基模型。2) MetaClaw (技能)：在基模型中加入技能驱动的快速适应；在每次失败的轨迹后，技能演化器会合成行为指令并立即注入系统提示词中，通过句子嵌入的余弦相似度进行前 k 检索。3) MetaClaw (完整)：结合了技能驱动的快速适应与通过强化学习（5天训练周期）进行机会性策略优化的完整流水线，仅针对 Kimi-K2.5 进行评估，因为它需要为目标骨干模型配置云端 LoRA 训练端点。所有条件均使用相同的提示词和工具集。该设计旨在隔离第 3 节中定义的两个 MetaClaw 组件各自的贡献。

                                                                                                                For the AutoResearchClaw evaluation, we deploy MetaClaw's skill-driven fast adaptation within AutoResearch-Claw's pipeline executor. After each pipeline run, failures and warnings from all 23 stages are captured as structured lessons and converted into reusable skill files via MetaClaw's lesson-to-skill evolver. On subsequent runs, accumulated skills are injected into the system prompt of all 18 LLM-driven stages. We run controlled A/B experiments with the same research topic, backbone LLM, and pipeline configuration, differing only in whether MetaClaw's skill injection is active. 对于 AutoResearchClaw 评估，我们将 MetaClaw 的技能驱动快速适应部署在 AutoResearch-Claw 的流水线执行器中。在每次流水线运行后，来自所有 23 个阶段的失败和警告会被捕获为结构化经验，并通过 MetaClaw 的经验转技能演化器转换为可重用的技能文件。在后续运行中，积累的技能会被注入到所有 18 个由大模型驱动阶段的系统提示词中。我们针对相同的研究主题、骨干大模型和流水线配置进行了受控的 A/B 测试，唯一的区别在于是否启用了 MetaClaw 的技能注入。

                                                                                                                4.2 Main Results

                                                                                                                4.2 主要结果

                                                                                                                Table 1 reports performance on both parts of MetaClaw-Bench for all five model-condition pairs. MetaClaw consistently improves over the respective baselines across both models, both adaptation modes, and both benchmark parts. 表 1 报告了所有五个模型-条件组合在 MetaClaw-Bench 两个部分上的表现。MetaClaw 在两个模型、两种适应模式以及两个基准测试部分中，均持续优于各自的基线。

                                                                                                                MetaClaw improves both models and the full pipeline yields the largest gains. Results are consistent across both benchmark parts. For GPT-5.2, MetaClaw (Skills) raises overall accuracy from 41.1% to 44.0% on Part I (+7.1% relative) and from 44.9% to 49.1% on Part II (+9.4% relative), with file-check completion rising from 14.7% to 17.1% on Part I and from 58.4% to 67.5% on Part II. For Kimi-K2.5, MetaClaw (Skills) improves accuracy from 21.4% to 28.3% on Part I (+32.2%) and from 21.1% to 26.9% on Part II (+27.5%). MetaClaw (Full) yields substantially larger gains: on Part I, accuracy reaches 40.6% and task completion rises 8.25 × (from 2.0% to 16.5%); on Part II, accuracy reaches 39.6% and file-check completion jumps from 18.2% to 51.9% (+185% relative). MetaClaw 提升了两个模型的效果，且完整流水线带来的增益最大。结果在两个基准测试部分中保持一致。对于 GPT-5.2，MetaClaw (技能) 将第一部分的整体准确率从 41.1% 提升至 44.0%（相对提升 7.1%），第二部分从 44.9% 提升至 49.1%（相对提升 9.4%），其中文件检查完成率在第一部分从 14.7% 提升至 17.1%，第二部分从 58.4% 提升至 67.5%。对于 Kimi-K2.5，MetaClaw (技能) 将第一部分的准确率从 21.4% 提升至 28.3%（+32.2%），第二部分从 21.1% 提升至 26.9%（+27.5%）。MetaClaw (完整) 带来了显著更大的增益：在第一部分，准确率达到 40.6%，任务完成率提升了 8.25 ×（从 2.0% 提升至 16.5%）；在第二部分，准确率达到 39.6%，文件检查完成率从 18.2% 跃升至 51.9%（相对提升 185%）。

                                                                                                                Stronger models benefit less and weaker models benefit more. GPT-5.2 starts from a higher baseline (41.1% vs. 21.4% on Part I), leaving less headroom for skill-driven gains. Kimi-K2.5, by contrast, lacks implicit procedural knowledge that the skill library provides explicitly, so skill injection yields larger returns. Notably, MetaClaw (Full) with Kimi-K2.5 (40.6%) nearly closes the gap with GPT-5.2's baseline (41.1%), demonstrating that the combination of skill injection and gradient-based policy optimization can largely compensate for model capability differences. This pattern suggests MetaClaw is particularly valuable for deploying capable but not state-of-the-art models at production scale. 较强的模型获益较少，较弱的模型获益更多。GPT-5.2 的基线较高（第一部分为 41.1% 对 21.4%），因此技能驱动带来的提升空间较小。相比之下，Kimi-K2.5 缺乏技能库所显式提供的隐性程序性知识，因此技能注入带来了更大的回报。值得注意的是，使用 Kimi-K2.5 的 MetaClaw (完整)（40.6%）几乎弥补了与 GPT-5.2 基线（41.1%）之间的差距，这表明技能注入与基于梯度的策略优化相结合，可以在很大程度上补偿模型能力上的差异。这一模式表明，MetaClaw 对于在生产规模上部署能力尚可但非顶尖的模型尤为有价值。

                                                                                                                The full pipeline unlocks end-to-end task completion and skills alone do not. On Part I, MetaClaw (Skills) leaves task completion rates unchanged for both models, confirming that skill injection sharpens partial execution quality without reliably enabling zero-defect outputs under heavy execution demands. MetaClaw (Full) closes this gap: Kimi-K2.5's completion rate jumps from 2.0% to 16.5% (8.25×). On Part II, where file-check tasks are rule-based,skills already drive a substantial completion gain (18.2%→33.8%), and the full pipeline pushes this further to 51.9%, confirming that weight-level optimization provides an additive benefit on top of skill injection regardless of task type. 完整流水线能够实现端到端的任务完成，而仅靠技能则无法做到。在第一部分，MetaClaw (技能) 对两个模型的任务完成率均无影响，这证实了技能注入虽然能提高部分执行质量，但在高执行需求下无法可靠地实现零缺陷输出。MetaClaw (完整) 弥补了这一差距：Kimi-K2.5 的完成率从 2.0% 跃升至 16.5%（8.25 倍）。在文件检查任务为规则导向的第二部分，技能本身已带来了显著的完成率提升（18.2%→33.8%），而完整流水线进一步将其推高至 51.9%，这证实了无论任务类型如何，权重层面的优化在技能注入之上都能提供额外的增益。

                                                                                                                Table 2 MetaClaw (Skills-Only) on AutoResearchClaw, a 23-stage autonomous research pipeline. Skill injection alone yields consistent improvements across all robustness metrics without requiring RL weight updates. 表 2 MetaClaw (仅技能) 在 AutoResearchClaw（一个 23 阶段的自主研究流水线）上的表现。仅进行技能注入即可在所有鲁棒性指标上实现持续改进，且无需进行强化学习权重更新。

                                                                                                                MetricBaseline+ MetaClaw (Skills)Relative Change
                                                                                                                Stage retry rate $\left( \downarrow \right)$10.5%7.9%↓ 24.8%
                                                                                                                Refine cycle count ( $\downarrow$ )2.01.2↓40.0%
                                                                                                                Pipeline stage completion $\left( \uparrow \right)$18/1919 / 19↑5.3%
                                                                                                                Composite robustness score $\left( \uparrow \right)$0.7140.845↑18.3%
                                                                                                                指标基线+ MetaClaw (技能)相对变化
                                                                                                                阶段重试率 $\left( \downarrow \right)$10.5%7.9%↓ 24.8%
                                                                                                                优化循环次数 ( $\downarrow$ )2.01.2↓40.0%
                                                                                                                流水线阶段完成率 $\left( \uparrow \right)$18/1919 / 19↑5.3%
                                                                                                                综合鲁棒性得分 $\left( \uparrow \right)$0.7140.845↑18.3%

                                                                                                                Since MetaClaw-Bench is an authored simulation rather than a collection of real user sessions (see Section 4), the absolute magnitudes of these gains are specific to this benchmark and may not transfer directly to production workloads. The primary value of these results lies in the consistent directional trends: skill-driven adaptation reliably improves partial execution quality across both models, while weight-level optimization is necessary to unlock end-to-end task completion. 由于 MetaClaw-Bench 是人工构建的模拟环境，而非真实用户会话的集合（见第 4 节），这些增益的绝对量级仅适用于该基准测试，可能无法直接迁移至生产工作负载。这些结果的主要价值在于其一致的方向性趋势：基于技能的自适应能够可靠地提升两种模型的局部执行质量，而权重层面的优化则是实现端到端任务完成的必要条件。

                                                                                                                MetaClaw generalizes to open-ended multi-stage pipelines. Table 2 reports MetaClaw's impact on AutoResearchClaw, an evaluation setting structurally different from MetaClaw-Bench. Using skills-only adaptation (no RL),MetaClaw reduces the stage retry rate by 24.8% (from 10.5% to 7.9%) and cuts refine cycles by 40.0% (from 2.0 to 1.2 per stage). Pipeline completion improves from 18/19 to 19/19 stages (+5.3%) , and the composite robustness score rises from 0.714 to 0.845 , an 18.3% improvement. These gains are achieved without any gradient-based policy updates, demonstrating that MetaClaw's lightweight, zero-downtime skill injection transfers effectively to complex, long-horizon agentic workflows beyond structured CLI tasks. MetaClaw 可泛化至开放式的多阶段流水线。表 2 展示了 MetaClaw 对 AutoResearchClaw 的影响，这是一个在结构上不同于 MetaClaw-Bench 的评估环境。仅使用技能自适应（无强化学习）时，MetaClaw 将阶段重试率降低了 24.8%（从 10.5% 降至 7.9%），并将精炼周期缩短了 40.0%（从每个阶段 2.0 次降至 1.2 次）。流水线完成度从 18/19 个阶段提升至 19/19 个阶段 (+5.3%)，综合鲁棒性得分从 0.714 提高到 0.845，提升了 18.3%。这些增益是在没有任何基于梯度的策略更新的情况下实现的，证明了 MetaClaw 轻量级、零停机的技能注入能够有效地迁移到结构化 CLI 任务之外的复杂长程智能体工作流中。

                                                                                                                4.3 Analysis

                                                                                                                4.3 分析

                                                                                                                Per-day accuracy trends. Figure 2 visualizes per-day accuracy (3-day rolling average) for all five conditions. Both models and all conditions show a consistent accuracy decline from day01-10 (where accuracies routinely exceed 50%) to day25-30 (where most models fall below 30%), confirming that MetaClaw-Bench exhibits increasing difficulty. MetaClaw's advantage over the baseline is most pronounced in the mid-range days (day11- 22), where tasks require multi-step procedural compliance that is learnable through failure distillation, and MetaClaw (Full) reaches its peak advantage of nearly 0.8 accuracy around day 19-20. The early days (day01-10) involve simpler manipulations where both conditions perform reasonably, and the late days (day23-30) are sufficiently complex that accumulated skills are insufficient without stronger model weights, leading all five conditions to converge toward similarly low performance. 每日准确率趋势。图 2 可视化了所有五种条件下的每日准确率（3 天滚动平均值）。两种模型在所有条件下均显示出从第 1-10 天（准确率通常超过 50%）到第 25-30 天（大多数模型降至 30% 以下）的一致准确率下降，证实了 MetaClaw-Bench 的难度在不断增加。MetaClaw 相较于基线的优势在中期（第 11-22 天）最为显著，此时任务需要通过失败蒸馏习得的多步程序合规性，MetaClaw (Full) 在第 19-20 天左右达到了近 0.8 准确率的峰值优势。早期（第 1-10 天）涉及较简单的操作，两种条件表现尚可；而后期（第 23-30 天）难度极大，若无更强的模型权重，积累的技能已不足以应对，导致所有五种条件最终趋向于同样较低的性能。

                                                                                                                Task-type breakdown. Figure 3 decomposes performance by task type, revealing that the two MetaClaw components address fundamentally different bottlenecks. Skills-only adaptation lifts multi-choice pass rates for both models while leaving file-check completion flat, as procedural knowledge helps reasoning but not execution. MetaClaw (Full) reverses this: Kimi-K2.5's file-check completion rate jumps to match GPT-5.2's baseline, while multi-choice accuracy slightly decreases as the policy shifts toward file-execution behavior during training. 任务类型细分。图 3 按任务类型分解了性能，揭示了 MetaClaw 的两个组件解决了本质上不同的瓶颈。仅技能自适应提升了两种模型的多选题通过率，但文件检查完成度保持不变，因为程序性知识有助于推理而非执行。MetaClaw (Full) 则扭转了这一局面：Kimi-K2.5 的文件检查完成率跃升至与 GPT-5.2 基线持平，而随着策略在训练期间向文件执行行为偏移，多选题准确率略有下降。

                                                                                                                RL training dynamics. Part II provides a fine-grained view of how policy optimization evolves over time. The file-check completion curve for MetaClaw (Full)-Kimi-K2.5 shows a clear inflection at day 8, after which per-day pass rates escalate rapidly: from ∼9% on days 1-4,through 27-36% on days 5-8,to 55-64% on days 9-10,and ultimately reaching 100% on days 12 and 14. This learning trajectory mirrors the MAML inner-loop update structure: the first several days accumulate support trajectories for skill synthesis and weight updates, the inflection marks when sufficient gradient signal has been collected for the LoRA fine-tune to shift the policy's execution strategy, and the late-phase convergence indicates that the policy has internalized the procedural rules surfaced by the skill library. The two-phase pattern (skill-driven gains first, RL-driven gains after day 8) directly validates the complementary timescale hypothesis underlying MetaClaw's design. 强化学习训练动态。第二部分提供了策略优化随时间演变的细致视图。MetaClaw (Full)-Kimi-K2.5 的文件检查完成曲线在第 8 天出现明显的拐点，此后每日通过率迅速攀升：从第 1-4 天的 ∼9%，到第 5-8 天的 27-36%，再到第 9-10 天的 55-64%，最终在第 12 天和第 14 天达到 100%。这一学习轨迹反映了 MAML 内循环更新结构：前几天积累了用于技能合成和权重更新的支持轨迹，拐点标志着已收集到足够的梯度信号供 LoRA 微调以改变策略的执行策略，而后期收敛表明策略已内化了技能库所揭示的程序规则。这种两阶段模式（先是技能驱动的增益，第 8 天后是强化学习驱动的增益）直接验证了 MetaClaw 设计背后的互补时间尺度假设。

                                                                                                                Skill library analysis. Across the 30-day session, MetaClaw's skill evolver synthesizes skills clustered around three recurring failure categories: (1) temporal format compliance, normalizing natural-language time expressions to ISO 8601 format with timezone offsets; (2) backup-before-modify protocol, creating .bak files before any destructive file operation; and (3) naming convention adherence, following date-prefixed file naming patterns (e.g., 20260408-. json). These cross-cutting behavioral heuristics generalize across tasks, explaining why a single failure can yield skills that improve performance on subsequent, structurally different questions. 技能库分析。在 30 天的会话中，MetaClaw 的技能演化器合成了围绕三个重复性失败类别的技能：(1) 时间格式合规性，将自然语言时间表达标准化为带有时区偏移的 ISO 8601 格式；(2) 修改前备份协议，在任何破坏性文件操作前创建 .bak 文件；(3) 命名规范遵循，遵循日期前缀的文件命名模式（例如 20260408-.json）。这些跨领域的行为启发式方法在不同任务间具有泛化性，解释了为什么单次失败产生的技能能够提升后续结构不同问题的性能。

                                                                                                                 

                                                                                                                Figure 2 Per-day accuracy over 30 simulated workdays (3-day rolling average). Solid lines: GPT-5.2; dashed lines: Kimi-K2.5. MetaClaw (Full) dominates in the mid phase (day 11-22) before difficulty outpaces accumulated knowledge in late days. 图 2 30 个模拟工作日的每日准确率（3 天滚动平均值）。实线：GPT-5.2；虚线：Kimi-K2.5。MetaClaw (Full) 在中期（第 11-22 天）占据主导地位，随后在后期因难度超过了积累的知识而表现下降。

                                                                                                                 

                                                                                                                Figure 3 Per-task-type pass rates. File-check (yellow) is unchanged by skills alone but jumps 8.25× under MetaClaw (Full). Multi-choice (blue) improves with skills but slightly decreases under MetaClaw (Full), reflecting a policy shift toward file-execution. 图3 各任务类型的通过率。文件检查（黄色）仅靠技能无法改变，但在MetaClaw（完整版）下跃升了8.25×。多项选择（蓝色）随技能提升，但在MetaClaw（完整版）下略有下降，反映了策略向文件执行的转移。

                                                                                                                Cross-domain skill transfer to AutoResearchClaw. The AutoResearchClaw results (Table 2) provide complementary evidence for skill generalization. In this setting, the skill evolver, designed for CLI-task adaptation, synthesizes actionable skills for a fundamentally different workload (multi-stage research automation) without any domain-specific tuning. The 40% reduction in refine cycles indicates that skills distilled from earlier pipeline failures (e.g., citation formatting errors, experiment code validation failures) directly prevent repeated mistakes in subsequent runs. This cross-domain transferability, combined with the zero-downtime deployment model (skill injection operates entirely at the prompt level), confirms that MetaClaw functions as a general-purpose continual learning layer applicable to diverse agentic systems. 跨领域技能迁移至AutoResearchClaw。AutoResearchClaw的结果（表2）为技能泛化提供了补充证据。在此设置中，专为CLI任务适配设计的技能演化器，无需任何领域特定微调，即可为截然不同的工作负载（多阶段研究自动化）合成可操作技能。精炼周期中40%的减少表明，从早期流水线故障（如引用格式错误、实验代码验证失败）中提炼的技能，直接防止了后续运行中重复犯错。这种跨领域迁移能力，结合零停机部署模型（技能注入完全在提示词层面运行），证实了MetaClaw可作为适用于多种智能体系统的通用持续学习层。

                                                                                                                Case studies. Table 3 contrasts two representative cases that illustrate the distinct contributions of the two MetaClaw mechanisms. In Case 1, a single distilled skill resolves a compliance error with zero weight update. In Case 2, skill injection provides necessary format context but is insufficient alone; weight-level RL updates are required to reliably execute a structurally complex file operation. 案例研究。表3对比了两个代表性案例，阐明了MetaClaw两种机制的不同贡献。在案例1中，单个提炼出的技能无需权重更新即可解决合规性错误。在案例2中，仅靠技能注入提供格式上下文是不够的；必须通过权重级RL更新，才能可靠地执行结构复杂的文件操作。

                                                                                                                5 Related Work

                                                                                                                5 相关工作

                                                                                                                Skill-based and memory-augmented agents. A line of work augments LLM agents with external memory or reusable skill libraries to improve performance without modifying model weights (Shinn et al., 2023; Zhao et al., 2024; Fang et al., 2025; Tang et al., 2025; Ouyang et al., 2025; Chhikara et al., 2025; Liu et al., 2026a; Wang et al., 2024b). Reflexion (Shinn et al., 2023) stores verbal self-reflections in an episodic buffer, allowing the agent to avoid repeating past mistakes. Mem0 (Chhikara et al., 2025) and SimpleMem (Liu et al., 2026a) maintain longer-horizon memory through hierarchical retrieval. On the skill side, Voyager (Wang et al.) incrementally builds a library of executable code skills from successful episodes, while ExpeL (Zhao et al., 2024) and Agent-KB (Tang et al., 2025) distills cross-task experience into natural-language rules. A key limitation shared by these methods is that the skill library (or memory) is treated as a static artifact (Xia et al., 2026): it is never coordinated with weight-level optimization, and successful trajectories are reused indiscriminately without regard for whether the agent's behavior has changed since they were collected. MetaClaw addresses both gaps by coupling skill evolution with policy optimization through explicit support-query separation. 基于技能和记忆增强的智能体。一系列研究通过外部记忆或可重用技能库增强LLM智能体，以在不修改模型权重的情况下提升性能（Shinn et al., 2023; Zhao et al., 2024; Fang et al., 2025; Tang et al., 2025; Ouyang et al., 2025; Chhikara et al., 2025; Liu et al., 2026a; Wang et al., 2024b）。Reflexion（Shinn et al., 2023）将口头自我反思存储在情景缓冲区中，使智能体能避免重蹈覆辙。Mem0（Chhikara et al., 2025）和SimpleMem（Liu et al., 2026a）通过分层检索维持长程记忆。在技能方面，Voyager（Wang et al.）从成功片段中增量构建可执行代码技能库，而ExpeL（Zhao et al., 2024）和Agent-KB（Tang et al., 2025）将跨任务经验提炼为自然语言规则。这些方法共同的一个关键局限是，技能库（或记忆）被视为静态工件（Xia et al., 2026）：它从未与权重级优化协同，且成功轨迹被不加区分地重用，而不考虑智能体行为自收集以来是否已发生变化。MetaClaw通过明确的支持-查询分离，将技能演化与策略优化相结合，弥补了这两项空白。

                                                                                                                Reinforcement learning for LLM agents. RLHF (Ouyang et al., 2022) and its variants establish the use of reward signals to fine-tune LLM behavior, and subsequent work applies RL to tool-using and agentic settings (Nakano et al., 2021; Yao et al., 2022). More recently, GRPO (Shao et al., 2024) and DAPO (Yu et al., 2025) demonstrate stable online policy gradient training for reasoning tasks (Schulman et al., 2017; Ahmadian et al., 2024; Shao et al., 2024; Feng et al., 2025; Zheng et al., 2025; Team et al., 2025; Dong et al., 2025). However, these approaches optimize a fixed policy against a fixed reward signal, with no mechanism for the agent to update its behavioral context between rollouts. In deployed interactive settings, they also do not address when to run training or which data remains valid for gradient updates after behavioral changes. MetaClaw LLM智能体的强化学习。RLHF（Ouyang et al., 2022）及其变体确立了利用奖励信号微调LLM行为的方法，后续研究将RL应用于工具使用和智能体场景（Nakano et al., 2021; Yao et al., 2022）。最近，GRPO（Shao et al., 2024）和DAPO（Yu et al., 2025）展示了针对推理任务的稳定在线策略梯度训练（Schulman et al., 2017; Ahmadian et al., 2024; Shao et al., 2024; Feng et al., 2025; Zheng et al., 2025; Team et al., 2025; Dong et al., 2025）。然而，这些方法针对固定奖励信号优化固定策略，缺乏智能体在多次运行间更新行为上下文的机制。在部署的交互式场景中，它们也未解决何时进行训练，或哪些数据在行为改变后仍对梯度更新有效的问题。MetaClaw

                                                                                                                Table 3 Representative case studies. Case 1 shows skill-driven fast adaptation (MetaClaw Skills, GPT-5.2); Case 2 shows the full pipeline (MetaClaw (Full), Kimi-K2.5). Both recover from score 0 to score 1.0; the mechanisms differ fundamentally. 表3 代表性案例研究。案例1展示了技能驱动的快速适配（MetaClaw Skills, GPT-5.2）；案例2展示了完整流水线（MetaClaw (Full), Kimi-K2.5）。两者均从得分0恢复至1.0；其机制存在本质区别。

                                                                                                                Model
                                                                                                                Day / Round
                                                                                                                Task type                                                                                                                		Case 1 MetaClaw (Skills)Case 2 MetaClaw (Full)
                                                                                                                GPT-5.2Kimi-K2.5
                                                                                                                Day 19 / Round 4Day 18 / Round 6
                                                                                                                File-checkFile-check
                                                                                                                Task instruction
                                                                                                                Update sprint8_board.json: set T-405/T- 406 to "done", add completed_at fields.Append a deployment record to deploy_log.json with fields timestamp (ISO 8601+TZ), env, status, and changes.
                                                                                                                Baseline response (score: 0)
                                                                                                                Reads file; directly overwrites it. Checker detects missing sprint8_board.json.bak $\rightarrow 0$ .Uses field name date instead of timestamp; omits changes. Checker rejects schema → 0.
                                                                                                                MetaClaw response (score: 1.0)
                                                                                                                Skill distilled from Day 2: "Always create .bak before modifying (P4)." Agent writes sprint8_board.json.bak, applies targeted patch. Checker passes $\rightarrow {1.0}$ .Skills inject "use ISO 8601 with timezone offset"; skills-only Kimi still omits changes array $\rightarrow 0$ . After RL: all four fields present, schema valid,backup created $\rightarrow {1.0}$ .
                                                                                                                Day accuracy (all rounds)
                                                                                                                Baseline: 43.9% MetaClaw (Skills): 62.1% Δ+18.2 ppBaseline: 8.3% Skills-only: 25.0% MetaClaw (Full): 80.6%
                                                                                                                Key mechanism
                                                                                                                Skills: one distilled rule generalizes across file types and subsequent days with zero weight update.RL: skills supply declarative format context; weight updates internalize the execution reliability that skill injection alone cannot enforce.
                                                                                                                模型
                                                                                                                日期 / 轮次
                                                                                                                任务类型                                                                                                                		案例 1 MetaClaw（技能）案例 2 MetaClaw（完整）
                                                                                                                GPT-5.2Kimi-K2.5
                                                                                                                第 19 天 / 第 4 轮第 18 天 / 第 6 轮
                                                                                                                文件检查文件检查
                                                                                                                任务指令
                                                                                                                更新 sprint8_board.json：将 T-405/T-406 设为“已完成”，添加 completed_at 字段。向 deploy_log.json 追加部署记录，包含 timestamp (ISO 8601+TZ)、env、status 和 changes 字段。
                                                                                                                基线响应（得分：0）
                                                                                                                读取文件；直接覆盖。检查器检测到缺少 sprint8_board.json.bak $\rightarrow 0$ 。使用字段名 date 代替 timestamp；遗漏 changes。检查器拒绝模式 → 0。
                                                                                                                MetaClaw 响应（得分：1.0）
                                                                                                                从第 2 天提炼的技能：“修改前始终创建 .bak (P4)”。智能体写入 sprint8_board.json.bak，应用目标补丁。检查器通过 $\rightarrow {1.0}$ 。技能注入“使用带时区偏移的 ISO 8601”；仅技能的 Kimi 仍遗漏 changes 数组 $\rightarrow 0$ 。强化学习后：四个字段齐全，模式有效，已创建备份 $\rightarrow {1.0}$ 。
                                                                                                                日准确率（所有轮次）
                                                                                                                基线：43.9% MetaClaw（技能）：62.1% Δ+18.2 pp基线：8.3% 仅技能：25.0% MetaClaw（完整）：80.6%
                                                                                                                关键机制
                                                                                                                技能：一条提炼出的规则可在不同文件类型和后续日期中泛化，无需权重更新。强化学习：技能提供声明式格式上下文；权重更新内化了仅靠技能注入无法强制执行的执行可靠性。

                                                                                                                targets exactly these practical constraints via opportunistic scheduling and skill generation versioning. 通过机会调度和技能生成版本控制，精准解决了这些实际约束。

                                                                                                                Continual and meta-learning. Meta-learning (Finn et al., 2017; Nichol et al., 2018; Hospedales et al., 2021) frames learning as optimizing for fast adaptation to new tasks, typically in an offline episode-based setting. Meta-reinforcement learning extends this idea to sequential decision-making: RL2 (Duan et al.,2016) trains a recurrent policy whose hidden state implicitly encodes task context, PEARL (Rakelly et al., 2019) infers a probabilistic context variable for off-policy adaptation, and ProMP (Rothfuss et al., 2019) applies trust-region constraints at the meta-level. These methods demonstrate effective fast adaptation in robotic control and navigation, but operate on simple network architectures with low-dimensional action spaces and assume fixed offline task distributions. Continual learning (Kirkpatrick et al., 2017; Lopez-Paz and Ranzato, 2017; Chaudhry et al., 2019; Zenke et al., 2017; Wang et al., 2024a, 2022) studies sequential task adaptation without forgetting through regularization, replay, or architectural strategies, yet does not incorporate fast adaptation mechanisms at inference time. Online meta-learning approaches (Finn et al., 2019; Nagabandi et al., 2018; Harrison et al., 2020; Yao et al., 2020) relax the offline assumption and even handle task heterogeneity, but remain grounded in representation learning over simple networks. MetaClaw extends the meta-learning objective to a non-stationary stream of LLM agent tasks where fast adaptation is gradient-free (skill synthesis in discrete natural-language space) and slow adaptation is gradient-based (policy optimization via RL), with a versioning protocol that preserves the support-query structure in an online, asynchronous setting. 持续学习与元学习。元学习（Finn 等人，2017；Nichol 等人，2018；Hospedales 等人，2021）将学习过程构建为针对新任务的快速适应优化，通常在离线的情景化设置中进行。元强化学习将这一理念扩展至序列决策：RL2（Duan 等人，2016）训练一种循环策略，其隐藏状态隐式编码了任务上下文；PEARL（Rakelly 等人，2019）推断出用于离线策略适应的概率上下文变量；ProMP（Rothfuss 等人，2019）在元层面应用了信赖域约束。这些方法在机器人控制和导航中展现了有效的快速适应能力，但均基于低维动作空间的简单网络架构，且假设任务分布在离线状态下是固定的。持续学习（Kirkpatrick 等人，2017；Lopez-Paz 和 Ranzato，2017；Chaudhry 等人，2019；Zenke 等人，2017；Wang 等人，2024a, 2022）研究如何在不遗忘的前提下通过正则化、重放或架构策略实现序列任务适应，但并未在推理阶段引入快速适应机制。在线元学习方法（Finn 等人，2019；Nagabandi 等人，2018；Harrison 等人，2020；Yao 等人，2020）放宽了离线假设，甚至能处理任务异构性，但仍局限于简单网络上的表征学习。MetaClaw 将元学习目标扩展至非平稳的 LLM 智能体任务流，其中快速适应是无梯度的（在离散自然语言空间中进行技能合成），而慢速适应是基于梯度的（通过强化学习进行策略优化），并采用版本控制协议，在在线异步环境中保留了支持集-查询集（support-query）结构。

                                                                                                                6 Conclusion

                                                                                                                6 结论

                                                                                                                We presented MetaClaw, a continual meta-learning framework that enables deployed LLM agents to improve autonomously through normal usage.
